disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Foot and lower leg appear profoundly pale with early mottling, reported/shown by nursing staff
trigger_question_examples: Can staff describe or show the colour of the foot and leg?
What does the skin look like right now?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Staff say it looks very pale, almost white, with some blotchy patches starting.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Progressive numbness (toe paraesthesia to complete anaesthesia) and inability to wiggle toes
trigger_question_examples: Can you feel your toes and foot?
Can you move your toes at all?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I can't feel my foot at all now, and I can't move my toes.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Known peripheral arterial disease with intermittent claudication, on clopidogrel and atorvastatin
trigger_question_examples: Does she have any history of poor circulation or leg cramping with walking?
What regular medications is she on?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, she has known poor circulation with cramping on walking, and she's on clopidogrel and atorvastatin.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Calf described by staff as firm/tense and very tender to touch
trigger_question_examples: How does the calf feel to touch — soft or firm/tense?
Is the calf very tender when touched?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Staff say the calf feels quite firm and it's extremely tender even to a light touch.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: The nurses have propped her leg up on a pillow to keep her comfortable, is that alright?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Should immediately correct this — the leg should be positioned dependent (hanging down/lower than the heart), not elevated, since elevation would further reduce arterial flow to an already ischaemic limb. Should not delay emergency escalation to address this.
